verses.text: The priests and Levites who first came up with Zerubbabel are recorded by their houses. The ministers of God are named with honour. The LORD keeps a roll of those who serve at His house from the first day of its restoring.

The high-priestly line is traced from Jeshua through Joiakim, Eliashib, Joiada, Jonathan, to Jaddua. The succession of God's ministers continues. He preserves a line to serve at His altar through the generations.

The high-priestly line is traced from Jeshua through Joiakim, Eliashib, Joiada, Jonathan, to Jaddua. The succession of God's ministers continues. He preserves a line to serve at His altar through the generations.

The heads of the priestly fathers' houses are listed, family by family, none omitted. Leadership in God's house is set in order. Each household of ministers has its appointed head and charge, and all are counted faithful.

The Levites and priests are recorded in their generations, with the gatekeepers keeping watch at the storerooms. Order and watchfulness mark the house. Faithful service keeps careful account and guards what is holy.

For the dedication they seek out the Levites and singers, who purify themselves, the people, the gates, and the wall. Joy is prepared by cleansing. Before God's people celebrate, they make themselves clean, for holiness fits the heart for praise.

For the dedication they seek out the Levites and singers, who purify themselves, the people, the gates, and the wall. Joy is prepared by cleansing. Before God's people celebrate, they make themselves clean, for holiness fits the heart for praise.

Nehemiah appoints two great companies to give thanks, one going right upon the wall in procession. The restored wall becomes a path of praise. What was built in danger is dedicated with thanksgiving to the God who gave it.

Nehemiah appoints two great companies to give thanks, one going right upon the wall in procession. The restored wall becomes a path of praise. What was built in danger is dedicated with thanksgiving to the God who gave it.

Nehemiah appoints two great companies to give thanks, one going right upon the wall in procession. The restored wall becomes a path of praise. What was built in danger is dedicated with thanksgiving to the God who gave it.

The first company, with priests blowing trumpets and singers, processes by the gates toward the house of God. Music and ministry lead the way. The work of God is crowned with worship, not mere celebration.

The first company, with priests blowing trumpets and singers, processes by the gates toward the house of God. Music and ministry lead the way. The work of God is crowned with worship, not mere celebration.

The first company, with priests blowing trumpets and singers, processes by the gates toward the house of God. Music and ministry lead the way. The work of God is crowned with worship, not mere celebration.
The second company of thanksgivers goes the opposite way upon the wall, the people following. The whole circuit rings with praise. United worship encircles the city God has restored.
The second company of thanksgivers goes the opposite way upon the wall, the people following. The whole circuit rings with praise. United worship encircles the city God has restored.

The two companies meet at the house of God, offering great sacrifices and rejoicing, 'for God had made them rejoice... so that the joy of Jerusalem was heard even afar off.' Their gladness overflows the walls. When God does great things, the joy of His people cannot be hidden.

They appoint men over the treasuries and portions for the priests and Levites, and the people gladly support those who serve, setting apart the holy things. Joy bears fruit in faithful provision. A thankful people takes care that God's ministers are not neglected.
